prerequisites: A running Metabase instance reachable over HTTP/HTTPS, with its API base URL (for example https://your-host/api) to set as instance_api_url.
A Metabase user account whose email and password you can use as username and password for authentication.
Network access from the connector to the Metabase instance API endpoint.

authMethods.name: Username and password (session login)
authMethods.summary: Provide your Metabase account email as username and your password. The connector exchanges these credentials at the POST /api/session endpoint to obtain a session token, which Metabase returns as an id. Maps to the username config field and the password secret field.

authMethods.name: Session token
authMethods.summary: Supply a pre-obtained Metabase session token directly via the session_token secret field instead of password. Session tokens are issued by POST /api/session and are sent on subsequent API requests in the X-Metabase-Session HTTP header.

setupSteps.title: Locate your Metabase API URL
setupSteps.body: Identify the base URL of your Metabase instance and append the API path. Set this as instance_api_url (for example https://metabase.example.com/api). This is a required field.

setupSteps.title: Provide account credentials
setupSteps.body: Set username to the email address of a Metabase user account. For credential-based login, also set the password secret field. The connector will POST these to /api/session to authenticate.

setupSteps.title: Or supply a session token directly
setupSteps.body: Instead of a password, you may obtain a session token by calling POST /api/session with your username and password; Metabase returns a session id. Put that value in the session_token secret field. Metabase sends this token in the X-Metabase-Session header on API requests.

sources.title: Working with the Metabase API (official learn guide)
